name: llamafile
tagline: Distribute and run a local LLM as a single executable file
seoTitle: llamafile - Run LLMs From a Single File
metaDescription: llamafile packages an LLM and its runtime into one executable that runs on most operating systems with no install. Download, chmod, and run a model locally.
overview: llamafile is a Mozilla project that turns an open large language model and the code to run it into a single executable file. It combines llama.cpp with Cosmopolitan Libc, so one file can run locally on most operating systems and CPU architectures without any installation step.

It is aimed at developers and end users who want to try or ship a local model without setting up a Python environment, downloading separate weights, or managing platform-specific builds. You download one file, make it executable, and run it.

As a local runtime, it fits the same niche as other on-device inference tools, but its focus is portability and zero-install distribution. The project also includes whisperfile, a single-file speech-to-text tool built on whisper.cpp and the same packaging.
features: Packages a model plus its runtime into one executable that needs no installation
Built on llama.cpp and Cosmopolitan Libc to run across most operating systems and CPU architectures
Pre-built llamafiles are available for download, ranging from small CPU-friendly models to larger ones
Includes whisperfile for single-file audio transcription and translation built on whisper.cpp
Runs on CPU out of the box, with support for larger models on more capable hardware and GPUs
Apache 2.0 licensed, with changes to llama.cpp and whisper.cpp kept under MIT for upstream compatibility

gettingStarted.intro: Download a pre-built llamafile, make it executable, and run it locally. On Windows, rename the file to add a .exe extension (only executables under 4GB run on Windows).

steps.heading: Download an example model
steps.body: Fetch a small pre-built llamafile (Qwen3.5 0.8B) so it works out of the box on most hardware.

steps.heading: Make it executable
steps.body: On macOS, Linux, or BSD, mark the downloaded file as executable.

steps.heading: Run it
steps.body: Start the llamafile. It launches the bundled server locally with no separate install.

useCases: Run a local LLM on a laptop without setting up Python, CUDA, or a package manager
Distribute a model to non-technical users as one file they can download and run
Test or demo open models across macOS, Linux, BSD, and Windows from the same binary
Transcribe or translate audio offline using whisperfile, the single-file speech-to-text companion
